_note: All model names live here. Change a model name in one place and the entire system updates. No code changes needed.

no_search_track._note: Activated when a group contains the N flag. These columns do not need web search — they are derived, calculated, or synthesised from context the model already has. The track has three tiers separated by upgrade flags.
no_search_track._note_trigger_flag: The presence of N in any column in the group puts the whole group on this track.
no_search_track._note_base: Default no-search model. Fast and cheap for straightforward synthesis. Used when neither C nor U is present.
no_search_track._note_upgrade_flags: Each of these flags counts as one upgrade step. C = complex synthesis requiring domain expertise. U = previous results were wrong, escalate. Two upgrades (C+U) reaches the top tier.
no_search_track._note_upgrade_table: upgrade_table[0] = one upgrade flag present, upgrade_table[1] = both upgrade flags present. Index is capped at len-1.

search_track._note: Default track for groups without N. Two sub-tiers before upgrades: qualitative columns use a lightweight search model; everything else uses the deep-search model. Upgrade flags push to the top tier.
search_track._note_qualitative_flag: If this flag is present and no upgrade flags are present, the column routes to base_qualitative. The LLM decides whether the expected answer is qualitative; the system decides what that means for model selection.
search_track._note_base_qualitative: Model used for Ql columns with no upgrades. Good at broad retrieval and summarisation. No deep reasoning or citation pinning needed.
search_track._note_base_default: Model used for untagged (precision/quantitative) columns with no upgrade flags. Perplexity-backed deep search with flash reasoning.
search_track._note_upgrade_flags: Same semantics as no_search_track: each flag is one upgrade step. On the search track both C and U land on the-clone-claude regardless of count — the table repeats the same value intentionally.
search_track._note_upgrade_table: Both one and two upgrades resolve to the-clone-claude. The table is kept symmetrical with no_search_track for consistency; a future top search tier can be added here without code changes.

qc._note: QC runs a second model over all group outputs together, catching cross-group inconsistencies and hallucinations that individual groups miss. It is only worthwhile when there are multiple groups to cross-check.
qc._note_min_search_groups: QC is disabled for single-group configs unless the P flag is present. With one group there is nothing to cross-check and QC adds cost without accuracy benefit (confirmed by benchmark). Exception: P = provenance required — QC is enabled to verify that claims are properly sourced, even in single-group configs.
qc._note_standard: Default QC model. Benchmark showed sonar-pro + gemini QC reaches 100% on hard science cells at 1.71c/cell — the best cost-accuracy point in the matrix.
qc._note_elevated: Elevated QC model used when any validation group is already running a claude model. Gemini QC cannot reliably catch errors made by claude-class models; opus can.
qc._note_elevated_if_model_contains: Substring match against all group model names. Matches claude-sonnet-4.6, claude-opus-4.6, and the-clone-claude (which routes through claude internally).
